CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-01-2019 Continued To Jan-08-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Transfer Action
Off Calendar Nov-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
G. Daniel Walker (B-54570/INF A-21) Plaintiff in Pro Per appearing telephonically on the Court's phone.
Hay-Mie Cho, Deputy Attorney General, 415-510-4433 of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the tentative ruling. Plaintiff argues San Francisco is the correct venue. Defendant did not recieve the first amended complaint. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SCOTT KERNAN, MICHAEL STAINER, N COLVIN, S MIRANDA, K GREEN, W. MUNIZ'S Motion To Transfer Action.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants' motion to transfer venue to Monterey County is granted. This is a prisoner's civil action alleging denial of access to the prison law library and the court system while plaintiff was incarcerated at Salinas Valley State Prison in Soledad, California, which is located in Monterey County. The proper county in which to bring suit against a public officer is the county in which the cause, or some part of the cause, arose, which in this case is Monterey County. (Code Civ. Proc. 393(b).)

In addition, Defendant is to pay transfer fees.

Order is signed. Order is with the Judge. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
